notes: Rows 0..3 are per-feature weights (order matches features array); row 4 is bias. Columns 0..2 are classes (healthy / watch / risk). Chosen so: (a) high readEditRatio drives healthy positive, (b) high contextPct + burnVelocity + reversals drive risk positive, (c) bias gives healthy a slight default preference on ambiguous inputs. Retrain with research/train-advisor-classifier.py against your own usage-history.jsonl.
